_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

There is **no base URL defined** in the `Ajax` helper itself. It only forwards whatever URL you pass as the first argument:

```15:26:design/demo (1)/index.js
var Ajax = {
    post: function (url, data, fn) {
        var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
        xhr.open("POST", url, true);
        xhr.setRequestHeader("Content-Type", "application/json");
        xhr.onreadystatechange = function() {
            if (xhr.readyState == 4 && (xhr.status == 200 || xhr.status == 304)) {
                fn.call(this, xhr.responseText);
            }
        };
        xhr.send(data);
    }
};
```

## How the URL is actually determined

Every call site passes a **root-relative path**, not a full URL:

| Endpoint | Where it's set |
|---|---|
| `/portal/getPortalPageSetting` | line 156 (on page load) |
| `/portal/auth` | line 169 (default submit) |
| `/portal/radius/auth` | line 275 |
| `/portal/ldap/auth` | line 273 |
| `/portal/sendSmsAuthCode` | line 377 |

Because those paths start with `/`, the browser resolves them against **the origin of the page you're viewing**:

```
{protocol}://{host}:{port}/portal/...
```

So if the portal HTML is served at `http://192.168.0.1/`, the first request goes to:

`http://192.168.0.1/portal/getPortalPageSetting`

## How to find it at runtime

1. **Browser address bar** — the origin of that URL is the base (e.g. `http://10.0.0.1:8080`).
2. **DevTools console** — run `window.location.origin`.
3. **Network tab** — reload the page and look at the POST to `getPortalPageSetting`; the full request URL is shown there.

## Contrast with the other portal file

Your `server-download/index (1).html` uses a different pattern: it builds an **absolute** URL from query-string params:

```17:17:server-download/index (1).html
    var submitUrl = getQueryString("scheme") + "://" + getQueryString("target") + ":" + getQueryString("targetPort") + "/portal/radius/auth";
```

That version is meant for a portal page hosted elsewhere, with `scheme`, `target`, and `targetPort` telling it where to POST. **`design/demo (1)/index.js` does not use that** — it assumes the page and `/portal/*` API live on the same host.

**Bottom line:** There is no config constant for the base URL in this file. The base is implicitly **whatever host serves the portal page**, and the API paths are always under `/portal/`.
